name: cli
context_prose:
cli is the composition root of the engram binary. It owns the targ Target definitions for every subcommand (recall, show, list, learn feedback/fact, update), holds the *Args structs, defines the thin I/O adapter shims (osDirLister, osFileReader, osStatExists, osWalkMd, osWalkSkills, osDirListMd, osMatchAny, makeAnthropicCaller, newTokenResolver, readAutoMemoryDirectorySetting), and wires those adapters into every pure-logic component (recall, memory, externalsources, anthropic, tokenresolver, tomlwriter). cli also embeds business-logic handlers for show, list, learn, and update — see the c3 Drift Note: those four are intended to live as peer packages alongside recall but currently live inside cli.

cli applies two universal CLI conventions before any subcommand runs business logic: it resolves an empty `--data-dir` to `$XDG_DATA_HOME/engram` (or `$HOME/.local/share/engram` when XDG is unset), and it resolves an empty `--project-slug` from cwd via `tr '/' '-'`. cli also enforces the SIGINT/SIGTERM force-exit-on-second-signal contract via SetupSignalHandling.
legend_items: Blue = peer components in the engram-cli-binary container
Solid R-edges = forward calls; dotted D-edges = DI back-edges (cli wires deps into peers)

edges.label: DI: createTemp, rename, mkdirAll, stat, remove
DI: StatFn, Reader, Walker, Matcher, Settings, DirLister, SkillFinder

properties.name: Subcommand surface is fixed
properties.statement: For all invocations of `Targets`, the returned target slice exposes exactly five top-level subcommands (recall, show, list, learn, update) with `learn` as a group of `feedback` and `fact`.
properties.notes: Architectural invariant; no test asserts the set or shape of returned targets.

properties.name: Empty data-dir resolves to XDG/HOME default
properties.statement: For all subcommand invocations with an empty `--data-dir`, `applyDataDirDefault` sets it to `$XDG_DATA_HOME/engram` if that env var is non-empty, otherwise to `$HOME/.local/share/engram`.
properties.notes: Tested via the pure helper `DataDirFromHome`; the wrapper `applyDataDirDefault` reads `os.Getenv`/`os.UserHomeDir` directly and is exercised end-to-end by every subcommand test.

properties.name: Empty project-slug derives from cwd
properties.statement: For all paths P, `ProjectSlugFromPath(P)` returns P with every `filepath.Separator` replaced by `-`, matching the shell `tr '/' '-'` convention.
properties.notes: applyProjectSlugDefault wraps this with an injected `getwd` so callers can stub cwd.

properties.name: Source flag must be human or agent
properties.statement: For all `learn fact`, `learn feedback`, and `update --source=…` invocations, `validateSource` returns `errInvalidSource` unless source is exactly `"human"` or `"agent"`.
properties.notes: Empty source is rejected for learn but accepted for update (update only validates when --source is supplied).

properties.name: Show requires a slug
properties.statement: For all `show` invocations with empty `--name`, `runShow` returns `errShowMissingSlug` without touching the filesystem.

properties.name: Show prints only non-empty fields
properties.statement: For all memory records M, `renderMemory(w, M)` writes a line for a field iff that field is a non-empty string on M.
properties.notes: Type, Source, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt and the type-specific content fields are independently gated.

properties.name: Show selects renderer by Type
properties.statement: For all memory records M, `renderMemoryContent` calls `renderFactContent` iff M.Type == "fact", otherwise `renderFeedbackContent` (so feedback fields render for any non-fact type, including empty).

properties.name: List swallows missing data dir
properties.statement: For all `list` invocations against a data dir where `ListAllMemories` returns `os.ErrNotExist`, `runList` returns nil and writes nothing.
properties.notes: First-run friendliness — an unprovisioned data dir is not an error.

properties.name: List output format is type | name | situation
properties.statement: For all stored memories M, `runList` writes one line per memory in the form `<type> | <name> | <situation>\n`.

properties.name: Learn skips dedup when no token
properties.statement: For all `learn fact`/`learn feedback` invocations where `resolveToken` returns the empty string, `makeConflictDeps` returns a nil caller and `checkForConflicts` short-circuits to (false, nil), so the write proceeds without an Anthropic call.

properties.name: Dedup API failure is non-fatal
properties.statement: For all `learn` invocations where the Haiku caller returns a non-nil error, `checkForConflicts` returns `(false, nil)` and the memory is still written.
properties.notes: Intentional: Anthropic outages must not block memory capture.

properties.name: Dedup conflict aborts write
properties.statement: For all `learn` invocations where Haiku reports a `DUPLICATE` or `CONTRADICTION` line and `--no-dup-check` is not set, `writeMemory` returns nil before invoking `tomlwriter.Write`, leaving the data dir unchanged.

properties.name: Dedup recognizes only NONE/DUPLICATE/CONTRADICTION
properties.statement: For all Haiku responses R, `parseConflictResponse(R, ...)` returns true iff at least one trimmed line begins with `DUPLICATE:` or `CONTRADICTION:`; the literal `NONE` and any other text return false.

properties.name: Learn output announces created name
properties.statement: For all successful `learn fact` / `learn feedback` writes, stdout receives a single trailing line `CREATED: <name>\n` where <name> is `memory.NameFromPath(filePath)`.

properties.name: Update preserves unspecified fields
properties.statement: For all `update` invocations and field F, if the corresponding `UpdateArgs` field is empty, `applyUpdateArgs` does not modify M[F].
properties.notes: Update is a sparse patch, not a full replacement.

properties.name: Update stamps UpdatedAt
properties.statement: For all successful `update` invocations, the persisted record's `UpdatedAt` field is set to `time.Now().UTC()` formatted as RFC3339.
properties.notes: Time source is not injected — tests assert format/non-empty rather than exact value.

properties.name: Update requires --name
properties.statement: For all `update` invocations missing `--name`, targ flag-parse returns an error and `runUpdate` is not called.

properties.name: Second signal forces exit
properties.statement: For all signal channels S, after `ForceExitOnRepeatedSignal` receives two values on S, it invokes `exitFn(ExitCodeSigInt)` (130). The first signal is allowed to flow through targ's context cancellation.

properties.name: SetupSignalHandling returns Targets
properties.statement: For all (stdout, stderr, stdin, exitFn) tuples, `SetupSignalHandling` returns `Targets(stdout, stderr, stdin)` after registering SIGINT/SIGTERM and starting the force-exit goroutine.
properties.notes: main.go relies on this to obtain the targ target slice.

properties.name: osDirLister filters to .jsonl files
properties.statement: For all directories D, `osDirLister.ListJSONL(D)` returns one `recall.FileEntry` per non-directory entry whose name has suffix `.jsonl`, and only those entries.
properties.notes: Stat failures are logged to stderr and silently skipped, not surfaced.

properties.name: osFileReader is a transparent os.ReadFile shim
properties.statement: For all paths P, `osFileReader.Read(P)` returns the same `(bytes, error)` pair as `os.ReadFile(P)` with no transformation.

properties.name: osMatchAny ignores Glob errors
properties.statement: For all glob slices G and cwd C, `osMatchAny(C)(G)` returns true iff at least one glob in G yields a non-empty `filepath.Glob` match under C; pattern errors are treated as no-match.
properties.notes: `**` patterns silently fail to match — known safe-failure mode.

properties.name: osDirListMd swallows missing dir
properties.statement: For all directories D that do not exist or fail to read, `osDirListMd(D)` returns `(nil, nil)` so DiscoverAutoMemory treats it as no files.
properties.notes: Auto-memory dir is opt-in; absent dir is the common case.

properties.name: osWalkMd is recursive and lossy
properties.statement: For all roots R, `osWalkMd(R)` returns absolute paths to every file under R with extension `.md`; permission and read errors on subtrees are silently skipped, not propagated.

properties.name: osWalkSkills filters to SKILL.md only
properties.statement: For all roots R, `osWalkSkills(R)` returns absolute paths only to files named exactly `SKILL.md` under R.

properties.name: Auto-memory settings precedence is local-then-user
properties.statement: For all home directories H, `readAutoMemoryDirectorySetting(H)()` returns the first non-empty `autoMemoryDirectory` it finds in `./.claude/settings.local.json` then `H/.claude/settings.json`, otherwise `("", false)`.

properties.name: Worktree detection falls back to empty
properties.statement: For all (ctx, cwd, home), `computeMainProjectDir` returns the empty string when cwd is not inside a git worktree distinct from the main checkout, when git is unavailable, or when `git rev-parse --git-common-dir` errors.

properties.name: haikuCallerAdapter pins Haiku model
properties.statement: For all (ctx, systemPrompt, userPrompt) tuples, `haikuCallerAdapter.Call` invokes the wrapped caller with model exactly `anthropic.HaikuModel` (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001).
properties.notes: Centralizes the model pin so subcommands cannot accidentally choose a different one.

properties.name: newSummarizer returns nil on empty token
properties.statement: For all empty token strings, `newSummarizer("")` returns nil, signalling the recall pipeline to skip LLM phases.

properties.name: discoverExternalSources never returns nil cache
properties.statement: For all (ctx, home), `discoverExternalSources` returns a non-nil `*externalsources.FileCache` so downstream readers can always call `cache.Read` safely.
properties.notes: Architectural invariant; no direct unit test — exercised indirectly via runRecallSessions.

properties.name: All cli I/O lives at this seam
properties.statement: For all packages under `internal/` other than `internal/cli`, no production source calls `os.*`, `exec.*`, `runtime.GOOS`, or `http.*` directly — all such calls are wired through DI from cli.
properties.notes: Architectural invariant from the project's DI-everywhere principle (CLAUDE.md). No mechanical lint enforces it; review-time discipline.
